slides.title: Real-World Examples
slides.subtitle: A map of what's out there to explore next
slides.narration: We built one MCP App from scratch in episode four, a small dice roller. This episode is about zooming back out: what does the official example gallery look like, and who's actually shipping this in production today. Consider this the map for where to go exploring on your own after the series wraps.

slides.heading: The official example gallery
slides.narration: The official ext-apps repository ships a genuinely wide gallery of example servers. Three-D and visualization, a CesiumJS globe, Three dot js scenes, shader effects. Data exploration tools like cohort heatmaps and customer segmentation. Business apps, scenario modelers, budget allocators. Media, PDF viewers, video, sheet music, even text-to-speech. Utilities like QR codes and system monitors. And starter templates in every major framework if you want to build your own instead of starting from scratch like we did in episode four.

slides.heading: Who's shipping this in production
slides.small: Launch partners named in the January 2026 MCP Apps announcement.
slides.narration: These aren't just demos, real companies were named as launch partners when MCP Apps was announced: Figma, Canva, Slack, Salesforce, Asana, Box, monday dot com, Clay, Hex, and Amplitude. Worth checking which of these have public MCP Apps integrations live by the time you're watching this, since this space is moving fast.

slides.heading: Series recap

items.text: MCP recap — why "Apps" got added at all
The protocol — lifecycle and security model
Claude Desktop's three distinct layers of support
A real MCP App, built from scratch

slides.narration: That closes out the series. We went from a plain MCP recap, through the actual protocol and its security model, to what Claude Desktop specifically supports, to building a real working MCP App from scratch, and finally this tour of the wider ecosystem. Everything, notes, slides, and the working dice server, is in the repo if you want to build on any of it yourself.
